channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Gojek co-founder, turned Indonesian Education Minister jailed for 10 years
item.description: Gojek co-founder and former Education Minister Nadiem Makarim has been sentenced to 10 years in prison.

item.title: Tutoring centre roof collapses killing 14 children in Pakistan
item.description: At least 14 children were killed when the roof of a private tutoring centre collapsed outside Lahore, Pakistan.

item.title: Supreme Court’s divided ruling on birthright citizenship may be revisited
item.description: The Supreme Court’s 6-3 ruling on birthright citizenship could signal that the issue could return to the Court.

item.title: World’s oceans experience hottest June ever, scientists say more heat ahead
item.description: European Union monitors say the first half of 2026 was 'marked by sustained and exceptional ocean warmth'.

item.title: Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 to reach World Cup last-16 and break 40-year curse
item.description: Adverse weather does not dampen Mexico's party as the cohosts storm into the last 16 by beating Ecuador.

item.title: NASA astronauts spacewalk to fix robotic arm
item.description: Two NASA astronauts stepping outside the International Space Station to fix robotic arm.

item.title: USA face Bosnia in World Cup knockouts, with pride, credentials on the line
item.description: Winning the whole tournament might be a stretch, but USA must look to regain momentum against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

item.title: Mercury’s Thomas says received online abuse following WNBA suspension
item.description: Mercury forward Alyssa Thomas was suspended for a game after making contact with her fist on Caitlin Clark's throat.

item.title: UN chief fears for ‘millions’ of Palestinians amid UNRWA funding shortfall
item.description: Antonio Guterres denounces 'disinformation, smear campaigns' against the UN agency for Palestinian refugees.

item.title: Iran says it couldn’t export a ‘single barrel of oil’ during US blockade
item.description: Iran was unable to export any oil during the US blockade of its ports, the country's chief negotiator has said.

item.title: US lifts restrictions on Anthropic’s powerful AI models Fable and Mythos
item.description: AI firm says it will begin restoring access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after the removal of export controls.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s homegrown artists are knocking K-pop off its pedestal
item.description: Drawing lessons from the global success of K-Pop, the region is finding its own voice in a new generation of artists.

item.title: Who is leading the race for the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot award?
item.description: Argentina's Messi is level on goals with France's Mbappe as World Cup favourites look to power through the knockouts.

item.title: Algeria to vote in test of post-Hirak political landscape
item.description: Algeria holds legislative elections amid debates over reform, turnout and political stability.

item.title: LeBron James thanks LA Lakers ahead of free agency exit for 24th season
item.description: After eight years at the LA Lakers, James's next destination will be decided in NBA's imminent free agency period.

item.title: Iran leaves World Cup with warm send-off from Mexican fans
item.description: Mexican fans gathered in Tijuana to bid farewell to Iran's national football team before the squad departed

item.title: North Korea’s Kim hails ‘unshakeable will’ to develop ties with China’s Xi
item.description: Kim Jong Un has sent Xi Jinping a congratulatory message to mark the 105th anniversary of the Chinese Communist Party.

item.title: Mbappe, Deschamps break World Cup records in Sweden thrashing
item.description: With his fifth and sixth goals at the tournament, Mbappe pulls even with Messi in the Golden Boot race.

item.title: Mexican fans keep Ecuador’s team awake before World Cup showdown
item.description: Mexican fans gathered outside Ecuador’s team hotel using loudspeakers, horns and motorcycles to disturb their rest.

item.title: Iran war live: Qatar’s PM meets US envoys; Tehran holds firm on conditions
item.description: Iran says talks on a final deal will not begin until hostilities end in Lebanon and US releases frozen Iranian funds.

item.title: ‘A rally like none other’: Trump unveils 2026 Republican midterm convention
item.description: The Republican Party hopes to retain control of both chambers of Congress in November's US midterm elections.

item.title: Donald Trump reports $1.4bn in cryptocurrency income in government filing
item.description: Trump has launched a slate of crypto-friendly policies since returning to the White House for a second term.

item.title: Venezuelans the US deported hours before earthquakes still missing
item.description: The US deported at least 146 people to Venezuela, just hours before two deadly earthquakes struck last Wednesday.

item.title: France beat Sweden as Mbappe draws level with Messi in World Cup goal chart
item.description: Kylian Mbappe and Bradley Barcola net, while Michael Olise wows, as France beat Sweden 3-0 to reach last 16.

item.title: “WhatsApp Privacy Changes”
item.description: WhatsApp is rolling out usernames to keep phone numbers private.
